title: Monday.com DEV - Tickets removed from sprint
excerpt:
I would love to be able to pull a report on tickets we removed from a sprint. I know in the AI breakdown after close of sprint, it will show how many tickets we removed from the sprint, but it doesn’t detail which ones. 
…

posts.author: tcraig (Tayla Craig)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would love to be able to pull a report on tickets we removed from a sprint. I know in the AI breakdown after close of sprint, it will show how many tickets we removed from the sprint, but it doesn’t detail which ones.

I assume that the AI pulls from this event in the logs for the tickets:

Is there somewhere I can pull a report on specific actions that happened?

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, you can pull a report on tickets removed from a sprint in Monday.com, but it requires using the Activity Log and exporting it manually.

Here’s how you can do it:

  1. Access the Board Activity Log:
  • Click the “Activity” button in the top-right corner of your board.
  • This will open a panel showing all changes made to the board, including item movements, status changes, and deletions

1

.
2. Filter the Log:

  • On Pro and Enterprise plans, you can filter the log by person, time, group, or column to narrow down to sprint-related changes

1

.

  • Look specifically for actions like “moved from group X to group Y” or status changes that indicate removal from a sprint.
  1. Export the Activity Log:
  • Click the Excel icon in the Activity Log panel to export the data

2

.

  • The exported file will include:
    • Date/time of change
    • User who made the change
    • Item name
    • Column affected
    • Previous value → New value

3
4. Identify Removed Tickets:

  • In the exported Excel file, filter for changes where items were moved out of the sprint group or had their sprint-related status changed.

Important Notes:

  • The Activity Log does not track item deletions or movements between boards, only changes within the same board.
  • If your team uses automations to manage sprints, those actions will also appear in the log with a robot icon
